title: Velvety Salty Filipino Chicken Stew
description: A comforting Filipino chicken stew that blends aromatic spices, full‑fat sour cream, and a velvety coconut milk base for a savory, silky finish.

ingredients.name: All‑purpose flour
ingredients.notes: dissolve in a splash of water before adding

instructions: Heat 2 Tbsp oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chicken thighs skin‑side down and sear until golden brown on both sides, about 5 minutes each side. Transfer the thighs to a plate and set aside.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger to the same pot. Sauté for 2–3 minutes until fragrant and the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook for another 3–4 minutes until the tomatoes soften.
Deglaze the pot with 1 Tbsp soy sauce, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the seared chicken thighs to the pot. Pour in the coconut milk and chicken broth, then bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25–30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Whisk 1 Tbsp flour with a small splash of water to create a slurry. Stir the slurry into the stew and let it thicken for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Lower the heat and stir in 1 cup full‑fat sour cream until the sauce is smooth and velvety. Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the pot from heat. Serve the stew hot over steamed jasmine rice or plain rice, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired. Enjoy!
